channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Hippo Builds Unified Claims Platform with Embedded AI
item.description: Hippo (San Jose, Calif.) is reflecting that shift in a unified, AI-enabled claims platform designed to support the full lifecycle of a claim while changing how work is distributed between systems and adjusters. “We’ve delivered a new unified claims platform that serves as a single operating system for our adjusters, a single source of truth for claims data,” says Kyle Ramsay, Chief Product Officer, Hippo. The platform provides “end-to-end AI assistance across the claims lifecycle, from intake to flagging to document generation and more,” Ramsay says, describing a system with AI embedded directly into workflow rather than deployed as a separate tool.

item.title: Ceasefire’s limited lifeline for insurers
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Standard Life launches private markets pension default
item.description: Standard Life has launched Future Opportunities, a new workplace pension default strategy that gives defined contribution members access to private markets as part of their retirement savings mix. The offering is available for trust-based products and is positioned as an alternative to the company’s existing Sustainable Multi Asset default, with a long-term target of around 25% in private market assets. The strategy will gradually add private equity, real assets, private debt, and venture capital, alongside public market holdings, with private markets specialist Future Growth Capital supporting asset selection and portfolio construction.

item.title: DXC Launches AI Workflow Apps to Streamline P&C Operations
item.description: Source: DXC.) DXC Technology (Ashburn, Va.) has introduced a suite of AI-driven workflow applications designed to help insurers modernize operations and reduce reliance on manual processes. “ By bringing together ServiceNow’s agentic AI and workflow technology and DXC’s deep insurance industry expertise, we’re redefining what’s possible in insurance operations ,” says Michael Park, SVP, Global Partnerships and Channels, ServiceNow. The Assure Smart Apps—which the company unveiled at its DXC Connect Insurance Executive Forum—are modular, pre-built applications that integrate with core systems to support claims, underwriting and customer engagement workflows, with implementation timelines of 12 weeks or less.

item.title: Munich Re Integrates Sixfold AI into Realytix Zero Platform
item.description: Munich Re (Munich) has integrated underwriting AI from Sixfold into its Realytix Zero platform to enhance automation across underwriting workflows. “Our partnership with Sixfold reflects the direction underwriting technology is taking,” says Florian Niklas, Co-Founder, Realytix Zero and Head, Underwriting Technologies, Munich Re. “By combining Realytix Zero’s product development and workbench capabilities with Sixfold’s risk analysis, insurers can get products into the market quickly, reduce manual review, and make underwriting decisions faster and more consistently.

item.title: ACORD Study Highlights Persistent M&A Execution Risk for Carriers
item.description: ACORD (New York) has found that nearly one-third of global carrier mergers and acquisitions destroy shareholder value, underscoring ongoing execution challenges despite shifting deal strategies. “For transactions that fell short, value destruction was driven primarily by execution, not deal logic,” says Dave Sterner, SVP, Research and Development, ACORD. The study, Carrier Mergers & Acquisitions: Drivers, Implications & Outcomes , which examined nearly 500 transactions across 84 countries between July 2023 and December 2025, found that 68 percent of deals created value, while 32 percent resulted in value destruction, based on total shareholder return relative to the MSCI World Index.

item.title: PacifiCorp Case Appellate Ruling Could Jeopardize $1B in Oregon Wildfire Victim Damages
item.description: An Oregon appeals court sided with PacifiCorp on Wednesday in a ruling that could jeopardize over $1 billion in damages for victims of the state’s devastating 2020 wildfires. In the ruling, a three-judge appellate panel found that the trial court erred in instructing the jury that it could assume that the evidence presented at trial regarding four different wildfires applied to all class members. The Oregon Court of Appeals sent the class-action case back to a lower trial court over concerns about a jury instruction given during a 2023 trial.

item.title: Meta Pulls Ads Aimed at Recruiting Plaintiffs for Social Media Addiction Lawsuits
item.description: Meta Platforms said on Thursday it is pulling ads from Facebook and Instagram aimed at recruiting new plaintiffs for ongoing litigation accusing it and other social media companies of designing their platforms to be addictive to young users. More than 3,300 lawsuits involving addiction claims are pending in California state court against Meta, Google, Snapchat parent Snap Inc., and ByteDance, TikTok’s parent company. The state court cases largely involve individuals suing the companies over claims that addiction to social media caused mental health harms.

item.title: Strong El Nino, Warmer Sea Impacts Atlantic Hurricane Season Forecasts
item.description: Moderate to strong El Niño events generally have a stronger tropical Atlantic vertical wind shear impact, and a warm Atlantic favors an above-average season, since a hurricane’s fuel source is warm ocean water, according to CSU researchers. Two new hurricane predictions forecast widely different storm totals for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season. Their April prediction comes with the caveat that it is “historically less accurate compared to those that follow in each season due to the considerable changes that can occur in the atmosphere and ocean between April and the peak of the Atlantic hurricane season from August to October.” Arizona State University’s College of Science Hydrology & Atmospheric Sciences Department, on the other hand, predicts nine hurricanes, four major hurricanes, and 20 named storms.

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: SiFive Leads With $400M For Custom Chip Designs As Aviation, Biotech And Defense Startups Also Raise Big
item.description: While no billion-dollar rounds led this week’s list, we nonetheless saw a variety of startups in industries ranging from semiconductors to aerospace to biotech raise sizable rounds. The week’s biggest deal was $400 million for SiFive, a semiconductor startup challenging incumbent Arm Holdings with chip designs built on an open rather than proprietary standard. This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S.

item.title: Climate conditioned ILW strategy can outperform: Reask / LGT ILS Partners research
item.description: According to a new working paper by catastrophe modelling specialist Reask and specialist insurance-linked securities investment manager LGT ILS Partners, a climate-conditioned Industry Loss Warranty (ILW) strategy can materially outperform traditional static modeling approaches. With this, Francesco Comola, Head of Analytics at LGT ILS Partners, along with colleagues at the firm whilst working alongside Bolliger and Reask’s CEO, Jamie Rodney, developed a climate-conditioned catastrophe modelling framework that combined two key components. The firms conducted a systematic back-test of a climate-conditioned strategy across a portfolio of 36 regional ILWs, spanning a 40-year period from 1985 to 2024.

item.title: Cat wind E&S rate decreases of 20%–30% becoming common amid capacity, competition: CRC
item.description: The catastrophe wind market is one of the most aggressively softening segments within the excess and surplus (E&S) lines property market, with rate decreases in the 20% to 30% range becoming “increasingly common”, according to wholesale insurance services provider CRC Group. For instance, rate decreases in the 20% to 30% range are increasingly common, making CAT wind one of the most aggressively softening segments within the property market,” CRC Group said. In its 2026 Property State of the Market report, CRC Group notes that the cat wind market remains highly competitive, with ample capacity, resulting in continued rate softening across most geographies.

item.title: Aon integrates new AI benchmarks into Radford McLagan Compensation Database
item.description: As demand for artificial intelligence (AI) talent and roles intensifies across industries, global insurance and reinsurance broking group Aon has launched enhancements to its Radford McLagan Compensation Database. By expanding the Radford McLagan Compensation Database with new AI job families and enhanced analytics, we’re helping leaders ground pay decisions in current, defensible market data — even as the market continues to evolve.” AI is rapidly changing work, demanding new skills that blend technical expertise with strategy, governance, risk, and operations. Based on insights from the Radford McLagan Compensation Database, core AI roles such as machine learning engineers, applied data and research scientists and AI platform engineers are among the fastest growing roles in the market, alongside rising demand for adjacent skills such as data engineering and cybersecurity.

item.title: Liberty Mutual establishes $600m endowment to support long-term community resilience
item.description: Sumar leads the Foundation’s long-term vision and strategy, and reports into Francis Hyatt, Chief Community Investments & Sustainability Officer, Liberty Mutual Insurance. “We have long believed that we have a responsibility to our communities and this endowment allows our commitment—and our impact—to continue for generations.” With the creation of the endowment, Liberty Mutual Foundation can expand its resources over time, enhance its ability to address emerging needs more effectively, and pursue multi-year, place-based, collaborative initiatives that drive meaningful, lasting outcomes. Liberty Mutual Insurance has announced the establishment of a $600 million endowment for Liberty Mutual Foundation to build stronger and more resilient communities.

item.title: Swiss Re’s shareholders elect Jean-Jacques Henchoz as new Board member and approve dividend
item.description: Jean‑Jacques Henchoz, the former Chief Executive Officer of large European reinsurer Hannover Re, has been elected as a new member of the Board of Directors of Swiss Re, another of Europe’s big four reinsurance companies. “I would like to thank our shareholders for their continued support and confidence in the Board of Directors, and congratulate Jean-Jacques Henchoz on his election to the Board of Directors,” said Swiss Re’s Chairman, Jacques de Vaucleroy. Alongside Henchoz’s addition to the Board, Swiss Re’s shareholders re-elected all proposed members of the Board of Directors, and also all proposed members of the Compensation Committee, each for one term of office.

item.title: Markel Expands in Australia With Office in Perth
item.description: Markel Insurance, the insurance operations within Markel Group Inc., announced the opening of a new office in Perth, Western Australia, with immediate effect. The new office supports Markel’s strategy to build a local operation across Australia, empowering underwriters to service the domestic business with on-the-ground expertise. “The opening of the Perth office reflects our continued focus on profitable growth in Australia.

item.title: Inszone Acquires Oklahoma’s Schuessler
item.description: Inszone Insurance Services announced its expansion in Oklahoma through the acquisition of Schuessler Insurance, Inc., a long-standing independent insurance agency based in Alva, Oklahoma. Founded in 1973 by Harvey Schuessler, the agency began as a small operation run from Schuessler’s dining room before opening its first office in 1975. Over the decades, Schuessler Insurance built a strong reputation for dependable service and deep community relationships.

item.title: Iowa AG Sues Meta Over Alleged Deceptive Practices on Instagram
item.description: Iowa Attorney General Brenna Bird announced she has filed a lawsuit against Meta and several of its subsidiaries, including Instagram and Facebook, for breaking Iowa’s consumer protection laws. The state also asserts that Meta designed Instagram to be addictive, particularly to Iowa youth, and that this addiction harms Iowa children by substantially affecting their health. The Attorney General’s Office alleges Instagram represents itself as being safe for Iowa’s children and teenagers, yet there is explicit content readily available.

item.title: Aviva enhances protection offering with AI-driven critical illness underwriting tool
item.description: The post Aviva enhances protection offering with AI-driven critical illness underwriting tool appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Aviva has expanded its AI-powered underwriting summarisation tool to individual critical illness applications, marking a further step in the use of generative AI across protection insurance. With the latest rollout, Aviva becomes the first insurer to apply the technology to critical illness underwriting.

item.title: Insurance Distributors Should Buy Carriers
item.description: Large insurance distributors are poised to acquire carriers as AI and private equity remove traditional barriers. Distributors should seek to acquire carriers and develop fit-for-purpose products and capabilities. Independent agents continue to drive premium growth for carriers, with 60% of life insurance sales and increasing annuity sales (46% through IA/IMO/Independent BD) coming from third-party distribution (TPD), according to LIMRA.

item.title: Ping An launches AI service upgrades under “Service Year 2026” initiative
item.description: Ping An has introduced upgrades to its AI-powered Express Service and Global Emergency Assistance as part of a broader “Service Year 2026” initiative aimed at improving customer experience and safety. The Express Service acts as a unified entry point across Ping An’s ecosystem, allowing users to complete transactions, claims, financing, and service requests through a single prompt. Ping An reported approximately 90 million monthly active users in 2025 and said it will continue expanding its service-driven model across financial, healthcare, and senior care offerings.

item.title: Crawford names Jemin Thakkar global CIO
item.description: Crawford & Company has appointed Jemin Thakkar as global chief information officer, effective April 1, 2026. Thakkar, who previously served as chief information security officer, will now lead the company’s global IT and technology organization and report to CEO Bruce Swain. Thakkar will also oversee Crawford’s approach to artificial intelligence, with an emphasis on governance, data discipline, and security.

item.title: GEICO highlights youth sports push tied to NCAA partnership
item.description: GEICO used its NCAA partnership to run a month-long campaign around youth sports access, culminating in Final Four events in Indianapolis and Phoenix. The effort included equipment donations through Good Sports and Boys & Girls Clubs of America , reaching communities across Houston, San Diego, Phoenix, and Indianapolis, along with funding for new scoreboards at select locations. As part of the campaign, GEICO also launched “Miles That Matter,” a docuseries on Paramount+ featuring athletes including Azzi Fudd and Napheesa Collier , focused on the role of community support in athletic development.

item.title: Seguros dentales: qué coberturas marcan la diferencia para el usuario
item.description: Si bien estas son las coberturas que marcan la diferencia en un seguro dental, existen otros aspectos que conviene valorar para elegir el mejor en cada caso. Esto hace que sea muy importante conocer los servicios que suelen valorar más los usuarios y marcan la diferencia a la hora de tomar la mejor decisión. En este sentido, alguno seguros dentales incluyen tarifas reducidas para implantes o precios especiales , lo que puede traducirse en un ahorro significativo.

item.title: Executives on the Move: New Claims Officers at Chubb, RLI
item.description: Chubb Limited announced that Kevin Rampe has been named senior vice president, Chubb Group, Global Claims Officer. In his expanded role, Rampe will lead the company’s claims organization globally and be responsible for all aspects of executive claims management, service and administration for Chubb through its worldwide network of claims offices. He will also retain his responsibilities as head of North America Claims.

item.title: Investors Press Amazon, Microsoft and Google on Water, Power Use in Data Centers
item.description: While Meta, Google, Amazon and Microsoft have all started using closed-loop cooling in their data centers that require much less water, the data on that usage varies. Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet’s Google have each recently abandoned construction of multibillion-dollar data centers over community opposition and now the companies are coming under shareholder pressure over the environmental impact of their projects. More than a dozen investors are cranking up the heat on companies ahead of annual shareholder meetings this spring, seeking more data on the tech giants’ water usage and conservation efforts as they seek to expand their computing power, according to interviews with Reuters.

item.title: AIG, Aon, Price Forbes, Pinnacle Underwriting, Aviso Specialty: 12 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon sees moderating of APAC W&I rate softening in 2026
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Ronnie Tong steps down as Swiss Re’s head of P&C facultative for Asia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Spotting risk earlier by tracking consumer credit journeys
item.description: By spotting those common patterns in consumers’ credit journeys early, it helps us prioritise groups of people and firms where financial stress is emerging. The PSD will further help us to plot trends in consumers' engagement across different credit products and identify triggers of financial distress across different consumer groups. That’s why we’ve been using richer datasets and sharper data science to drive better outcomes in the consumer finance market, widen financial inclusion, and support economic growth.

item.title: Fintech Startups Globally Raise More Money In Far Fewer Deals In Q1 2026
item.description: U.S.-based startups have historically raised more fintech funding than any other country in the world, and the first quarter of 2026 was no different. Global venture funding to financial technology startups totaled $12 billion across 751 deals in 2026 as of April 6, per Crunchbase data . However, sequentially, the $12 billion raised is down 33% compared to the fourth quarter of 2025, when fintech startups raised $17.8 billion globally.

item.title: TSR reduces hurricane season forecast in April update, citing moderate to strong El Nino
item.description: Tropical Storm Risk (TSR) has now issued its April forecast update for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season , reducing its extended range numbers from December and citing an expectation of moderate to strong El Nino conditions. “The primary factor influencing the lowering of the TSR April forecast for 2026 North Atlantic hurricane activity to be around 40% below the 1991-2020 climatology is the anticipated moderate or strong El Nino conditions through summer and autumn 2026. TSR’s April forecast update now calls for 12 named tropical storms, 5 hurricanes and just 1 major hurricane for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Palomar seeks $375m Cal-quake & Hawaii named storm cover with Torrey Pines Re 2026-1
item.description: Palomar Insurance Holdings has returned to the catastrophe bond market to sponsor its seventh issuance, with an initial $375 million target for a source of capital markets backed California earthquake and Hawaii named storm reinsurance from a new Torrey Pines Re Ltd. Palomar has now returned and is seeking both California earthquake and Hawaii named storm protection with its seventh in the Torrey Pines Re program of catastrophe bonds, we have now learned. Three of the tranches of notes will give Palomar capital markets backed reinsurance protection against California earthquake losses, on an indemnity and per-occurrence basis, while a fourth tranche targets Hawaii named storm reinsurance again on a per-occurrence and indemnity trigger basis for the sponsor.

item.title: KatRisk expands global cat modelling capabilities through RED acquisition
item.description: Furthermore, KatRisk explained that its acquisition of RED reinforces its commitment to advancing innovation, deepening technical expertise, and expanding global reach. According to KatRisk, the acquisition of RED accelerates the development of a high-fidelity U.S. By combining their established capabilities in North America and Europe, KatRisk and RED seek to expedite the creation and provision of a highly accurate U.S.

item.title: CopperPoint names Kellen Booher as President and CEO in planned leadership transition
item.description: CopperPoint Mutual Insurance Holding Company, a provider of workers’ compensation and commercial insurance, has appointed Kellen Booher as its new President and Chief Executive Officer, marking the next phase in the company’s leadership evolution. Prior to CopperPoint, Booher held senior leadership roles at Chubb, most recently as Executive Vice President and Head of Consumer & Small Commercial for major international markets. Booher joined CopperPoint in 2025 as President and Chief Operating Officer, where he led operational performance across the organization and supported key strategic initiatives.

item.title: Evercore ISI flags mounting pressures across P&C insurance
item.description: Evercore ISI, the investment research arm of Evercore, has outlined a challenging backdrop for the property and casualty (P&C) insurance sector in its first-quarter 2026 preview, pointing to increasing cyclical pressure across commercial, personal, and reinsurance markets. While casualty pricing is expected by Evercore ISI to provide some support against softness in property and other short-tail lines, the firm cautions that sustained strong returns across the industry could encourage increased competition. Evercore ISI also notes that broader inflationary pressures, including social inflation, may prevent the cycle from deteriorating as sharply as in previous periods, potentially shortening the duration of the downturn while still allowing for acceptable returns.

item.title: Miguel Araújo to lead Iberia business at Gallagher Re
item.description: Gallagher & Co., has announced the appointment of Miguel Araújo as the new leader of its Iberia business. Gallagher Re, the reinsurance broking and advisory arm of Arthur J. Araújo brings a combination of technical expertise and commercial experience, supporting the development of solutions and the management of client relationships.

item.title: Dubai’s DIFC Introduces Temporary Economic Support for Business, Retail Community
item.description: Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) said on Thursday it was introducing a package of temporary relief measures to support its business and retail community “as the region emerges from the current environment.” The measures, effective immediately, include flexible payment plans for commercial and retail tenants, installment options for license renewal fees, and grace periods on certain administrative payments related to lease contracts, company registration, data protection filings and employee pension enrolment, a DIFC statement said. “We stand alongside our clients, partners and employees with a clear commitment to provide support and reassurance when it is needed most,” DIFC Authority Chief Executive Arif Amiri said. DIFC is home to 8,844 active firms, including more than 1,052 regulated entities spanning banks, asset managers, insurers and brokerages, according to the center.

item.title: Zurich Austria Launches Branch in Poland
item.description: Zurich Austria has launched a branch in Poland, marking Zurich Insurance Group’s entry into the Polish corporate insurance market. The Polish branch is located in Warsaw and is managed from Austria under the leadership of Kurt Möller, chief underwriting officer Commercial, Austria & CEE, and member of the board of Zurich Austria. “Zurich’s entry into the corporate insurance business in Poland marks another strategic milestone for us.

item.title: Abbott Jury Awards at Least $53 Million in Infant-Formula Trial
item.description: Abbott Laboratories said it faces a jury award of at least $53 million over claims by four mothers that the company hid risks its premature-infant formula can cause a bowel disease which creates serious health issues for frail babies. Jurors in state court in Chicago on Thursday found Abbott knew premature babies could develop necrotizing enterocolitis, or NEC, by ingesting the company’s cow-milk-based formula, but continued to market the product to hospital intensive-care units. After awarding the mothers compensation for their losses, the jury will convene Friday to determine whether to impose punitive damages on Abbott.

item.title: Aviva expands AI underwriting tool to critical illness
item.description: Aviva is expanding its AI-powered underwriting summary tool to individual critical illness applications. With this move, Aviva is the first insurer to apply the technology to critical illness underwriting. The tool supports front-end underwriting by helping assess applications at submission, and also enables post-application audits.

item.title: Stable goes offline after raising $112 million
item.description: About a year after its $60 million Series B , Stable expanded its price risk platform into the energy, fertilizer, and packaging sectors . Commodity price insurtech Stable appears to have gone dark, with its website offline and its X (Twitter) account deactivated. Stable raised $6 million in seed funding in 2019, followed by a $46.5 million Series A in 2021 and a $60 million Series B in 2022, bringing total funding to about $112.5 million.

item.title: Experian adds LV= to Support Hub
item.description: Experian has added LV= to its Support Hub platform, expanding the network of financial services firms using the tool to capture and share customer support needs. Support Hub is a free service that allows individuals to communicate preferences such as communication methods, need for extra time, or assistance during interactions across multiple providers through a standardized profile. The addition of LV= extends Support Hub’s reach into life and pensions, with the insurer aiming to improve accessibility and tailor interactions based on customer needs.

item.title: Eiopa plantea la posibilidad de un pool europeo para gestionar grandes catástrofes naturales
item.description: El primero sería un pool europeo de seguros frente a catástrofes naturales, financiado mediante primas basadas en el riesgo, que permitiría diversificar exposiciones entre países y tipos de peril. En este contexto, Eiopa y el Mecanismo Europeo de Estabilidad (ESM) han publicado un documento de discusión en el que analizan un posible enfoque europeo de reparto del riesgo para mejorar la gestión de los eventos naturales extremos. El documento no plantea un diseño cerrado, sino que busca alimentar el debate regulatorio y político sobre cómo afrontar de forma más coordinada el impacto financiero de las catástrofes naturales en Europa.

item.title: HDI Global ficha a Anna Dinarés como suscriptora senior de RC General
item.description: Anna Dinarés López acaba de incorporarse a HDI Global como suscriptora senior en el Departamento de Responsabilidad Civil General. Su trayectoria profesional incluye 14 años en AIG como suscriptora de RC General y CPI (Contaminación de Productos), EIL-Responsabilidad Medioambiental y RC Sanitaria en España. En el año 2018 pasó a Axa XL como suscriptora de RC General y RC Sanitaria y desde 2022 lideraba el ramo de RC General y RC Sanitaria en España de Everest .

item.title: La gestión de siniestros, principal reto para la innovación en el sector asegurador
item.description: El sector asegurador español afrontó en 2025 un punto de inflexión en la percepción de innovación por parte de los consumidores, tras alcanzar en 2024 su mejor resultado histórico. Los elementos que más penalizan la percepción global de innovación no son tanto el precio, sino factores experienciales como la atención al cliente, la transparencia en las condiciones y, de forma destacada, la gestión de los siniestros. Así lo reflejan las conclusiones de la IV edición del Índice Español de Innovación (ÍEI), presentado por la Universidad Carlos III de Madrid y la consultora Neovantas a partir de más de 25.000 encuestas anuales a consumidores.

item.title: The Doctors Company Names Hayes Interim SVP of Claims
item.description: The Doctors Company named Brittnie Hayes interim senior vice president of claims. She will also set the strategic and operational direction for The Doctors Company claims and strengthen reserve governance and litigation strategy. She joined The Doctors Company in 2023, and has held national and regional leadership roles, most recently as vice president of claims for the Northeast region.

item.title: Most Workers Saw Reduced Symptoms Using Injury-Prevention Tech, Study Shows
item.description: Workers who use technology to prevent musculoskeletal disorders on the job reduced concern about injury, improved posture and greater awareness of risks that lead to pain and strain, according to a study by the National Safety Council. The report, Frontline Worker Perceptions of MSD Prevention Technology , shows nearly 70% of respondents said they experience job-related musculoskeletal disorder symptoms. Devices that provide direct physical support, such as exoskeletons and robots, were most strongly associated with reduced symptoms.

item.title: HDI Global appoints Everest’s Diane Chong as senior underwriter for downstream energy
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Allianz hires Bryce Hatton as head of motor fleet underwriting in Australia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles
